search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG239 B12 deficiency symptoms risks tests follow-up official
site:dha.gov.ae vitamin B12 deficiency guideline UAE
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic deficiency diagnosis from an unspecified result
medicine replacement route, dose, or frequency
adult guidance applied to people under 16
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Result type and clinical context are required.
Medication regimens are outside this documentation-only mapping.
NG239 covers people aged 16 and over.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People aged 16 and over with suspected or confirmed vitamin B12 deficiency; younger people and condition-specific pregnancy, neurological, haematological, and malabsorption contexts need tailored evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE primary-care, laboratory, prescribing, referral, safeguarding, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact recognition section supports interval neurological, cognitive, visual, fatigue, anaemia, diet, medicine, surgery, and functional context without assuming symptoms are caused by B12 deficiency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports variable and nonspecific symptom context, neurological, cognitive, visual, fatigue, anaemia, diet, medicine, surgery and other risk-factor documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact testing section supports clinician review of test type, pregnancy or nitrous-oxide context, supplements, reference range, and indeterminate results without autonomously interpreting the result.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording the type of vitamin B12 test, pregnancy or nitrous-oxide context, current supplements, laboratory reference context, indeterminate results and clinician-led further assessment without autonomously interpreting a result.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact follow-up section supports symptom response, worsening or new symptoms, adherence context, and clinician-agreed reassessment without mapping replacement route, dose, or frequency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports symptom response, worsening or new symptoms, adherence context and clinician-agreed reassessment or follow-up. Medicine route, dose and frequency are excluded from mappings.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact result, reference range, symptoms, cause, pregnancy, medicine, replacement route or dose, adherence, response, diagnosis, further testing, referral, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
